name: Osteogenesis Imperfecta (Brittle Bone Disease)
description: Osteogenesis imperfecta is a rare hereditary collagen defect causing abnormally fragile bones that fracture with minimal trauma. Affected puppies sustain fractures with routine handling and manipulation.
treatment_overview: Bisphosphonates (pamidronate) can strengthen bones but do not cure the condition. Fracture management with appropriate fixation. Severely affected puppies are often euthanised on welfare grounds.
prevention_tips: DNA test available for Golden Retriever form
